---
format: html
page-navigation: false
---


::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}

<div class="bio-card">
  <img src="https://www.shoalsmarinelaboratory.org/sites/default/files/styles/max_width_1200px/public/media/2023-11/easton-white.webp?itok=lmd6LBgg" alt="Easton White">
  <h4>Easton White</h4>
  <p><i> (he/him)</i> </p>
  <h6>Assistant Professor<br>
  Department of Biology<br>
  University of New Hampshire (UNH)</h6>

  <p>Dr. Easton White is a quantitative marine ecologist who uses mathematical and statistical tools, coupled with experiments and field observations, to answer questions in ecology, conservation science, sustainability, and ecosystem management. Most of his work is focused on marine systems, especially fisheries and spatial planning. Easton currently conducts research on assessing the effectiveness of protected area networks, improving species monitoring programs, and modeling socio-ecological systems in the context of fisheries. His work centers on how environmental variability, in particular rare events (e.g., hurricanes, COVID-19 pandemic), affects ecosystems and those that depend on them.</p>
</div>

:::

::: {.g-col-6}

<div class="bio-card">
  <img src="images/frings_headshot.png" alt="Faith Frings">
  <h4>Faith Frings </h4>
  <p><i>(she/her)</i></p>
  <h6>Integrative Biology PhD Candidate<br> 
  Department of Biological Sciences<br>
  University of New Hampshire</h6>
  <p>Faith Frings (she/her) is a discipline-based researcher (DBER) interested in student motivation and cognition in statistics and data science in the undergraduate life sciences. She is currently a Ph.D. candidate at the University of New Hampshire researching mechanisms underlying the development of student motivation and engagement in coding in R in life sciences courses. She uses multilevel modeling and structural equation modeling to test the impacts of factors on student motivation and engagement in R-integrated biology courses. She has three years of experience teaching graduate and undergraduate students data science and statistics in R as a teaching assistant for Dr. Easton White at the University of New Hampshire. </p>
</div>  

:::

:::
